description: REQ-FEED-009 EARS clause requires the pending-switch indicator to be displayed 'WHEN FeedViewState.pendingNextNoteId !== null である (すなわち editingStatus ∈ {switching, save-failed})'. The implementation reduces this to a single check `viewState.pendingNextNoteId === noteId` with no editingStatus guard. While the spec rationale notes the domain invariant guarantees `pendingNextNoteId` is null outside switching/save-failed, the impl is brittle: any defect or test fixture that breaks the domain invariant (e.g., a malformed FeedDomainSnapshot fixture during regression testing or an upstream bug in EditingSessionState) will silently surface a false 'switching' indicator while the user is editing or idle. Defense-in-depth absent. Test FeedRow.dom.vitest.ts:257-267 only verifies absence when pendingNextNoteId is null; never asserts absence when status='editing' AND pendingNextNoteId === noteId.
recommendation: Tighten predicate to: `viewState.pendingNextNoteId === noteId && (viewState.editingStatus === 'switching' || viewState.editingStatus === 'save-failed')`. Add a DOM test that asserts the indicator is NOT shown when editingStatus='editing' but pendingNextNoteId === noteId.
